    53	    /// Only created when using the copy-to-fixed-allocator approach
    54	    /// (both import plugin and JS plugins enabled).

    59	    /// The values are module records of sections (check the docs of `ProcessedModule.section_module_records`)
    60	    /// Its entries are kept across groups because modules discovered in former groups could be referenced by modules in latter groups.
    61	    ///
    62	    /// `ModuleRecord` is a cyclic data structure.
    63	    /// To make sure all `ModuleRecord` gets dropped after `Runtime` is dropped,
    64	    /// `modules_by_path` must own `ModuleRecord` with `Arc`, all other references must use `Weak<ModuleRecord>`.
    65	    modules_by_path: ModulesByPath,
    66	    /// Collected disable directives from linted files

    70	/// Output of `Runtime::process_path`
    71	struct ModuleProcessOutput<'alloc_pool> {
    72	    /// All paths in `Runtime` are stored as `OsStr`, because `OsStr` hash is faster
    73	    /// than `Path` - go checkout their source code.
    74	    path: Arc<OsStr>,
    75	    processed_module: ProcessedModule<'alloc_pool>,
    76	}
    77	
    78	/// A module processed from a path
    79	#[derive(Default)]
    80	struct ProcessedModule<'alloc_pool> {
    81	    /// Module records of source sections, or diagnostics if parsing failed on that section.
    82	    ///
    83	    /// Modules with special extensions such as .vue could contain multiple source sections (see `PartialLoader::PartialLoader`).
    84	    /// Plain ts/js modules have one section. Using `SmallVec` to avoid allocations for plain modules.
    85	    section_module_records: SmallVec<[Result<ResolvedModuleRecord, Vec<OxcDiagnostic>>; 1]>,
    86	
    87	    /// Source code and semantic of the module.
    88	    ///
    89	    /// This value is required for linter to run on the module.  There are two cases where `content` is `None`:
    90	    /// - Import plugin is enabled and the module is a dependency, which is processed only to construct the module graph, not for linting.
    91	    /// - Couldn't get the source text of the module to lint, e.g. the file doesn't exist or the source isn't valid utf-8.
    92	    ///
    93	    /// Note that `content` is `Some` even if parsing is unsuccessful as long as the source to lint is valid utf-8.
    94	    /// It is designed this way to cover the case where some but not all the sections fail to parse.
    95	    content: Option<ModuleContent<'alloc_pool>>,
    96	}
    97	
    98	struct ResolvedModuleRequest {
    99	    specifier: CompactStr,
   100	    resolved_requested_path: Arc<OsStr>,
   101	}
   102	
   103	/// ModuleRecord with all specifiers in import statements resolved to real paths.
   104	struct ResolvedModuleRecord {
   105	    module_record: Arc<ModuleRecord>,
   106	    resolved_module_requests: Vec<ResolvedModuleRequest>,
   107	}
   108	
   109	self_cell! {
   110	    struct ModuleContent<'alloc_pool> {
   111	        owner: AllocatorGuard<'alloc_pool>,
   112	        #[not_covariant]
   113	        dependent: ModuleContentDependent,
   114	    }
   115	}
   116	struct ModuleContentDependent<'a> {
   117	    source_text: &'a str,
   118	    section_contents: SectionContents<'a>,
   119	}
   120	
   121	// Safety: dependent borrows from owner. They're safe to be sent together.
   122	unsafe impl Send for ModuleContent<'_> {}
   123	
   124	/// source text and semantic for each source section. They are in the same order as `ProcessedModule.section_module_records`
   125	type SectionContents<'a> = SmallVec<[SectionContent<'a>; 1]>;
   126	struct SectionContent<'a> {
   127	    source: JavaScriptSource<'a>,
   128	    /// None if section parsing failed. The corresponding item with the same index in
   129	    /// `ProcessedModule.section_module_records` would be `Err(Vec<OxcDiagnostic>)`.
   130	    semantic: Option<Semantic<'a>>,
   131	    /// Parser tokens for the section.
   132	    /// Empty if section parsing failed, or if token collection was not requested (no JS plugins).
   133	    parser_tokens: ArenaBox<'a, [Token]>,
   134	}
   135	
   136	/// A module with its source text and semantic, ready to be linted.
   137	///
   138	/// A `ModuleWithContent` is generated for each path in `runtime.paths`. It's basically the same
   139	/// as [`ProcessedModule`], except `content` is non-Option.
   140	struct ModuleToLint<'alloc_pool> {
   141	    path: Arc<OsStr>,
   142	    section_module_records: SmallVec<[Result<Arc<ModuleRecord>, Vec<OxcDiagnostic>>; 1]>,
   143	    content: ModuleContent<'alloc_pool>,
   144	}
   145	impl<'alloc_pool> ModuleToLint<'alloc_pool> {
   146	    fn from_processed_module(
   147	        path: Arc<OsStr>,
   148	        processed_module: ProcessedModule<'alloc_pool>,
   149	    ) -> Option<Self> {
   150	        processed_module.content.map(|content| Self {
   151	            path,
   152	            section_module_records: processed_module
   153	                .section_module_records
   154	                .into_iter()
   155	                .map(|record_result| record_result.map(|ok| ok.module_record))
   156	                .collect(),
   157	            content,
   158	        })
   159	    }
   160	}
   161	
   162	/// A simple trait for the `Runtime` to load and save file from a filesystem
   163	/// The `Runtime` uses OsFileSystem as a default
   164	/// The Tester and `oxc_language_server` would like to provide the content from memory
   165	pub trait RuntimeFileSystem {
   166	    /// reads the content of a file path
   167	    ///
   168	    /// # Errors
   169	    /// When no valid path is provided or the content is not valid UTF-8 Stream
   170	    fn read_to_arena_str<'a>(
   171	        &self,
   172	        path: &Path,
   173	        allocator: &'a Allocator,
   174	    ) -> Result<&'a str, std::io::Error>;
   175	
   176	    /// write a file to the file system
   177	    ///
   178	    /// # Errors
   179	    /// When the program does not have write permission for the file system
   180	    fn write_file(&self, path: &Path, content: &str) -> Result<(), std::io::Error>;
   181	}
   182	
   183	pub struct OsFileSystem;
   184	
   185	impl RuntimeFileSystem for OsFileSystem {
   186	    fn read_to_arena_str<'a>(
   187	        &self,
   188	        path: &Path,
   189	        allocator: &'a Allocator,
   190	    ) -> Result<&'a str, std::io::Error> {
   191	        read_to_arena_str(path, allocator)
   192	    }
   193	
   194	    fn write_file(&self, path: &Path, content: &str) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
   195	        fs::write(path, content)
   196	    }
   197	}
   198	
   199	impl Runtime {
   200	    pub(super) fn new(linter: Linter, options: LintServiceOptions) -> Self {
   201	        // If global thread pool wasn't already initialized, do it now.
   202	        // This "locks" config for the thread pool, which ensures `rayon::current_num_threads()`
   203	        // cannot change from now on.
   204	        //
   205	        // Initializing the thread pool without specifying `num_threads` produces a threadpool size
   206	        // based on `std::thread::available_parallelism`. However, Rayon's docs state that:
   207	        // > In the future, the default behavior may change to dynamically add or remove threads as needed.
   208	        // https://docs.rs/rayon/1.11.0/rayon/struct.ThreadPoolBuilder.html#method.num_threads
   209	        //
   210	        // However, I (@overlookmotel) assume that would be considered a breaking change,
   211	        // so we don't have to worry about it until Rayon v2.
   212	        // When Rayon v2 is released and we upgrade to it, we'll need to revisit this and make sure
   213	        // we still guarantee that thread count is locked.
   214	        //
   215	        // If thread pool was already initialized, this won't do anything.
   216	        // `build_global` will return `Err` in that case, but we can ignore it.
   217	        // That just means the config (and so number of threads) is already locked.
   218	        // https://docs.rs/rayon/1.11.0/rayon/struct.ThreadPoolBuilder.html#method.build_global
   219	        let _ = rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().build_global();
   220	
   221	        let thread_count = rayon::current_num_threads();
   222	
   223	        // Create allocator pools.
   224	        //
   225	        // * If both JS plugins and import plugin enabled, use copy-to-fixed-allocator approach.
   226	        //   This approach is to use standard allocators for parsing/linting (lower memory usage),
   227	        //   and copy ASTs to a fixed-size allocator only when passing to JS plugins.
   228	        //
   229	        // * If JS plugins, but no import plugin, use fixed-size allocators for everything.
   230	        //   Without import plugin, there's no danger of memory exhaustion, as no more than <thread count>
   231	        //   ASTs are live at any given time.
   232	        //
   233	        // * If no JS plugins, use standard allocators for parsing/linting.
   234	        #[cfg(all(target_pointer_width = "64", target_endian = "little"))]
   235	        let (allocator_pool, js_allocator_pool) = if linter.has_external_linter() {
   236	            if options.cross_module {
   237	                (
   238	                    AllocatorPool::new(thread_count),
   239	                    Some(AllocatorPool::new_fixed_size(thread_count)),
   240	                )
   241	            } else {
   242	                (AllocatorPool::new_fixed_size(thread_count), None)
   243	            }
   244	        } else {
   245	            (AllocatorPool::new(thread_count), None)
   246	        };
   247	
   248	        #[cfg(not(all(target_pointer_width = "64", target_endian = "little")))]
   249	        let allocator_pool = AllocatorPool::new(thread_count);
   250	
   251	        let resolver = options.cross_module.then(|| Self::get_resolver(options.tsconfig));
   252	
   253	        Self {
   254	            allocator_pool,
   255	            #[cfg(all(target_pointer_width = "64", target_endian = "little"))]
   256	            js_allocator_pool,
   257	            cwd: options.cwd,
   258	            linter,
   259	            resolver,
   260	            modules_by_path: papaya::HashMap::builder()
   261	                .hasher(BuildHasherDefault::default())
   262	                .resize_mode(papaya::ResizeMode::Blocking)
   263	                .build(),
   264	            disable_directives_map: Arc::new(Mutex::new(FxHashMap::default())),
   265	        }
   266	    }
   267	
   268	    /// Get [`AllocatorPool`] for copying ASTs to fixed-size allocators, if one is required.
   269	    fn js_allocator_pool(&self) -> Option<&AllocatorPool> {
   270	        #[cfg(all(target_pointer_width = "64", target_endian = "little"))]
   271	        let pool = self.js_allocator_pool.as_ref();
   272	
   273	        #[cfg(not(all(target_pointer_width = "64", target_endian = "little")))]
   274	        let pool = None;
   275	
   276	        pool
   277	    }
   278	
   279	    pub fn set_disable_directives_map(
   280	        &mut self,
   281	        map: Arc<Mutex<FxHashMap<PathBuf, DisableDirectives>>>,
   282	    ) {
   283	        self.disable_directives_map = map;
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    fn get_resolver(tsconfig_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Resolver {
   287	        use oxc_resolver::{
   288	            ResolveOptions, TsconfigDiscovery, TsconfigOptions, TsconfigReferences,
   289	        };
   290	        let tsconfig = match tsconfig_path {
   291	            Some(path) if path.is_file() => Some(TsconfigDiscovery::Manual(TsconfigOptions {
   292	                config_file: path,
   293	                references: TsconfigReferences::Auto,
   294	            })),
   295	            Some(_) => None, // Path provided but file doesn't exist
   296	            None => Some(TsconfigDiscovery::Auto),
   297	        };
   298	        let extension_alias = tsconfig.as_ref().map_or_else(Vec::new, |_| {
   299	            vec![
   300	                (".js".into(), vec![".js".into(), ".ts".into()]),
   301	                (".mjs".into(), vec![".mjs".into(), ".mts".into()]),
   302	                (".cjs".into(), vec![".cjs".into(), ".cts".into()]),
   303	            ]
   304	        });
   305	        Resolver::new(ResolveOptions {
   306	            extensions: VALID_EXTENSIONS.iter().map(|ext| format!(".{ext}")).collect(),
   307	            main_fields: vec!["module".into(), "main".into()],
   308	            condition_names: vec!["module".into(), "import".into()],
   309	            extension_alias,
   310	            tsconfig,
   311	            ..ResolveOptions::default()
   312	        })
   313	    }
   314	
   315	    fn get_source_type_and_text<'a>(
   316	        file_system: &(dyn RuntimeFileSystem + Sync + Send),
   317	        path: &Path,
   318	        ext: &str,
   319	        allocator: &'a Allocator,
   320	    ) -> Option<Result<(SourceType, &'a str), Error>> {
   321	        let source_type = SourceType::from_path(path);
   322	        let not_supported_yet =
   323	            source_type.as_ref().is_err_and(|_| !LINT_PARTIAL_LOADER_EXTENSIONS.contains(&ext));
   324	        if not_supported_yet {
   325	            return None;
   326	        }
   327	
   328	        let mut source_type = source_type.unwrap_or_default();
   329	        // Treat JS and JSX files to maximize chance of parsing files.
   330	        if source_type.is_javascript() {
   331	            source_type = source_type.with_jsx(true);
   332	        }
   333	
   334	        let file_result = file_system.read_to_arena_str(path, allocator).map_err(|e| {
   335	            Error::new(OxcDiagnostic::error(format!(
   336	                "Failed to open file {} with error \"{e}\"",
   337	                path.display()
   338	            )))
   339	        });
   340	        Some(match file_result {
   341	            Ok(source_text) => Ok((source_type, source_text)),
   342	            Err(e) => Err(e),
   343	        })
   344	    }
   345	
   346	    /// Prepare entry modules for linting.
   347	    ///
   348	    /// `on_module_to_lint` is called for each entry modules in `paths` when it's ready for linting,
   349	    /// which means all its dependencies are resolved if import plugin is enabled.
   350	    fn resolve_modules<'a>(
   351	        &'a self,
   352	        file_system: &'a (dyn RuntimeFileSystem + Sync + Send),
   353	        paths: &'a IndexSet<Arc<OsStr>, FxBuildHasher>,
   354	        scope: &Scope<'a>,
   355	        check_syntax_errors: bool,
   356	        tx_error: Option<&'a DiagnosticSender>,
   357	        on_module_to_lint: impl Fn(&'a Self, ModuleToLint) + Send + Sync + Clone + 'a,
   358	    ) {
   359	        if self.resolver.is_none() {
   360	            paths.par_iter().for_each(|path| {
   361	                let output =
   362	                    self.process_path(file_system, paths, path, check_syntax_errors, tx_error);
   363	                let Some(entry) =
   364	                    ModuleToLint::from_processed_module(output.path, output.processed_module)
   365	                else {
   366	                    return;
   367	                };
   368	                on_module_to_lint(self, entry);
   369	            });
   370	            return;
   371	        }
   372	        // The goal of code below is to construct the module graph bootstrapped by the entry modules (`paths`),
   373	        // and call `on_entry` when all dependencies of that entry is resolved. We want to call `on_entry` for each
   374	        // entry as soon as possible, so that the memory for source texts and semantics can be released early.
   375	
   376	        // Sorting paths to make deeper paths appear first.
   377	        // Consider a typical scenario:
   378	        //
   379	        // - src/index.js
   380	        // - src/a/foo.js
   381	        // - src/b/bar.js
   382	        // ..... (thousands of sources)
   383	        // - src/very/deep/path/baz.js
   384	        //
   385	        // All paths above are in `paths`. `src/index.js`, the entrypoint of the application, references
   386	        // almost all the other paths as its direct or indirect dependencies.
   387	        //
   388	        // If we construct the module graph starting from `src/index.js`, contents (sources and semantics) of
   389	        // all these paths mus<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    25	
    26	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq)]
    27	pub enum OutputFormat {
    28	    Default,
    29	    /// GitHub Check Annotation
    30	    /// <https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-commands-for-github-actions#setting-a-notice-message>
    31	    Github,
    32	    Gitlab,
    33	    Json,
    34	    Unix,
    35	    Checkstyle,
    36	    Stylish,
    37	    JUnit,
    38	}
    39	
    40	impl FromStr for OutputFormat {
    41	    type Err = String;
    42	
    43	    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
    44	        match s {
    45	            "json" => Ok(Self::Json),
    46	            "default" => Ok(Self::Default),
    47	            "unix" => Ok(Self::Unix),
    48	            "checkstyle" => Ok(Self::Checkstyle),
    49	            "github" => Ok(Self::Github),
    50	            "gitlab" => Ok(Self::Gitlab),
    51	            "stylish" => Ok(Self::Stylish),
    52	            "junit" => Ok(Self::JUnit),
    53	            _ => Err(format!("'{s}' is not a known format")),
    54	        }
    55	    }
    56	}
    57	
    58	/// Some extra lint information, which can be outputted
    59	/// at the end of the command
    60	pub struct LintCommandInfo {
    61	    /// The number of files that were linted.
    62	    pub number_of_files: usize,
    63	    /// The number of lint rules that were run. If the number varies and can't be clearly
    64	    /// computed, then this defaults to None.
    65	    pub number_of_rules: Option<usize>,
    66	    /// The used CPU threads count
    67	    pub threads_count: usize,
    68	    /// Some reporters want to output the duration it took to finished the task
    69	    pub start_time: Duration,
    70	}
    71	
    72	/// An Interface for the different output formats.
    73	/// The Formatter is then managed by [`OutputFormatter`].
    74	trait InternalFormatter {
    75	    /// Print all available rules by oxlint
    76	    fn all_rules(&self, _enabled_rules: FxHashSet<&str>) -> Option<String> {
    77	        None
    78	    }
    79	
    80	    /// At the end of the Lint command the Formatter can output extra information.
    81	    fn lint_command_info(&self, _lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
    82	        None
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    /// oxlint words with [`DiagnosticService`](oxc_diagnostics::DiagnosticService),
    86	    /// which uses a own reporter to output to stdout.
    87	    fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter>;
    88	}
    89	
    90	pub struct OutputFormatter {
    91	    internal: Box<dyn InternalFormatter>,
    92	}
    93	
    94	impl OutputFormatter {
    95	    pub fn new(format: OutputFormat) -> Self {
    96	        Self { internal: Self::get_internal_formatter(format) }
    97	    }
    98	
    99	    fn get_internal_formatter(format: OutputFormat) -> Box<dyn InternalFormatter> {
   100	        match format {
   101	            OutputFormat::Json => Box::<JsonOutputFormatter>::default(),
   102	            OutputFormat::Checkstyle => Box::<CheckStyleOutputFormatter>::default(),
   103	            OutputFormat::Github => Box::new(GithubOutputFormatter),
   104	            OutputFormat::Gitlab => Box::<GitlabOutputFormatter>::default(),
   105	            OutputFormat::Unix => Box::<UnixOutputFormatter>::default(),
   106	            OutputFormat::Default => Box::new(DefaultOutputFormatter),
   107	            OutputFormat::Stylish => Box::<StylishOutputFormatter>::default(),
   108	            OutputFormat::JUnit => Box::<JUnitOutputFormatter>::default(),
   109	        }
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    /// Print all available rules by oxlint
   113	    /// See [`InternalFormatter::all_rules`] for more details.
   114	    pub fn all_rules(&self, enabled_rules: FxHashSet<&str>) -> Option<String> {
   115	        self.internal.all_rules(enabled_rules)
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    /// At the end of the Lint command we may output extra information.
   119	    pub fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
   120	        self.internal.lint_command_info(lint_command_info)
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    /// Returns the [`DiagnosticReporter`] which then will be used by [`DiagnosticService`](oxc_diagnostics::DiagnosticService)
   124	    /// See [`InternalFormatter::get_diagnostic_reporter`] for more details.
   125	    pub fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
   126	        self.internal.get_diagnostic_reporter()
   127	    }
   128	}

   130	#[cfg(test)]
   131	mod test {
   132	    use crate::tester::Tester;
   133	
   134	    const TEST_CWD: &str = "fixtures/cli/output_formatter_diagnostic";
   135	
   136	    #[test]
   137	    fn test_output_formatter_diagnostic_formats() {
   138	        let mut formats: Vec<&str> =
   139	            vec!["checkstyle", "default", "github", "junit", "stylish", "unix"];
   140	
   141	        // disabled for windows
   142	        // json will output the offset which will be different for windows
   143	        // when there are multiple lines (`\r\n` vs `\n`)
   144	        if cfg!(not(target_os = "windows")) {
   145	            formats.push("json");
   146	        }
   147	
   148	        // Exclude `gitlab` on big-endian systems because fingerprints differ there
   149	        if cfg!(not(target_endian = "big")) {
   150	            formats.push("gitlab");
   151	        }
   152	
   153	        for fmt in &formats {
   154	            let args_vec = [format!("--format={fmt}"), "test.js".to_string()];
   155	            let args_ref: Vec<&str> = args_vec.iter().map(std::string::String::as_str).collect();
   156	            Tester::new().with_cwd(TEST_CWD.into()).test_and_snapshot(&args_ref);
   157	        }
   158	    }
   159	
   160	    #[test]

   211	    // Test that each of the formatters can output the disable directive violations.
   212	    #[test]
   213	    fn test_output_formatter_diagnostic_formats_with_disable_directive() {
   214	        let mut formats: Vec<&str> =
   215	            vec!["checkstyle", "default", "github", "junit", "stylish", "unix"];
   216	
   217	        // disabled for windows
   218	        // json will output the offset which will be different for windows
   219	        // when there are multiple lines (`\r\n` vs `\n`)
   220	        if cfg!(not(target_os = "windows")) {
   221	            formats.push("json");
   222	        }
   223	
   224	        // Exclude `gitlab` on big-endian systems because fingerprints differ there
   225	        if cfg!(not(target_endian = "big")) {
   226	            formats.push("gitlab");
   227	        }
   228	
   229	        for fmt in &formats {
   230	            let args_vec = [
   231	                format!("--format={fmt}"),
   232	                "--report-unused-disable-directives".to_string(),
   233	                "disable-directive.js".to_string(),
   234	            ];
   235	            let args_ref: Vec<&str> = args_vec.iter().map(std::string::String::as_str).collect();
   236	            Tester::new().with_cwd(TEST_CWD.into()).test_and_snapshot(&args_ref);
   237	        }
   238	    }

     2	
     3	use oxc_diagnostics::{
     4	    Error, Severity,
     5	    reporter::{DiagnosticReporter, DiagnosticResult, Info},
     6	};
     7	
     8	use super::default::get_diagnostic_result_output;
     9	use crate::output_formatter::InternalFormatter;
    10	
    11	#[derive(Debug)]
    12	pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
    13	
    14	impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
    15	    fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
    16	        Box::new(GithubReporter)
    17	    }
    18	}
    19	
    20	/// Formats reports using [GitHub Actions
    21	/// annotations](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-commands-for-github-actions#setting-an-error-message). Useful for reporting in CI.
    22	struct GithubReporter;
    23	
    24	impl DiagnosticReporter for GithubReporter {
    25	    fn finish(&mut self, result: &DiagnosticResult) -> Option<String> {
    26	        Some(get_diagnostic_result_output(result))
    27	    }
    28	
    29	    fn supports_minified_file_fallback(&self) -> bool {
    30	        false
    31	    }
    32	
    33	    fn render_error(&mut self, error: Error) -> Option<String> {
    34	        Some(format_github(&error))
    35	    }
    36	}
    37	
    38	fn format_github(diagnostic: &Error) -> String {
    39	    let Info { start, end, filename, message, severity, rule_id } = Info::new(diagnostic);
    40	    let severity = match severity {
    41	        Severity::Error => "error",
    42	        Severity::Warning | miette::Severity::Advice => "warning",
    43	    };
    44	    let title = rule_id.map_or(Cow::Borrowed("oxlint"), Cow::Owned);
    45	    let filename = escape_property(&filename);
    46	    let message = escape_data(&message);
    47	    format!(
    48	        "::{severity} file={filename},line={},endLine={},col={},endColumn={},title={title}::{message}\n",
    49	        start.line, end.line, start.column, end.column
    50	    )
    51	}
    52	
    53	fn escape_data(value: &str) -> String {
    54	    // Refs:
    55	    // - https://github.com/actions/runner/blob/a4c57f27477077e57545af79851551ff7f5632bd/src/Runner.Common/ActionCommand.cs#L18-L22
    56	    // - https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/fe3e7ce9a7f995d29d1fcfd226a32bca407f9dc8/packages/core/src/command.ts#L80-L94
    57	    let mut result = String::with_capacity(value.len());
    58	    for c in value.chars() {
    59	        match c {
    60	            '\r' => result.push_str("%0D"),
    61	            '\n' => result.push_str("%0A"),
    62	            '%' => result.push_str("%25"),
    63	            _ => result.push(c),
    64	        }
    65	    }
    66	    result
    67	}
    68	
    69	fn escape_property(value: &str) -> String {
    70	    // Refs:
    71	    // - https://github.com/actions/runner/blob/a4c57f27477077e57545af79851551ff7f5632bd/src/Runner.Common/ActionCommand.cs#L25-L32
    72	    // - https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/fe3e7ce9a7f995d29d1fcfd226a32bca407f9dc8/packages/core/src/command.ts#L80-L94
    73	    let mut result = String::with_capacity(value.len());
    74	    for c in value.chars() {
    75	        match c {
    76	            '\r' => result.push_str("%0D"),
    77	            '\n' => result.push_str("%0A"),
    78	            ':' => result.push_str("%3A"),
    79	            ',' => result.push_str("%2C"),
    80	            '%' => result.push_str("%25"),
    81	            _ => result.push(c),
    82	        }
    83	    }
    84	    result
    85	}

   135	            .with_label(Span::new(0, 1300))
   136	            .with_source_code(NamedSource::new("file://test.ts", source_text));
   137

   139	        sender.send(vec![diagnostic]).unwrap();
   140	        drop(sender);

   144	        let output = String::from_utf8(output).unwrap();

   148	        assert!(!output.contains("File is too long to fit on the screen"));
   149	        assert!(!output.contains("file=,line=0,endLine=0,col=0,endColumn=0"));
   150	    }

     1	use std::time::Duration;
     2	
     3	use crate::output_formatter::InternalFormatter;
     4	use oxc_diagnostics::{
     5	    Error, GraphicalReportHandler,
     6	    reporter::{DiagnosticReporter, DiagnosticResult},
     7	};
     8	use oxc_linter::table::RuleTable;
     9	use rustc_hash::FxHashSet;
    10	
    11	#[derive(Debug)]
    12	pub struct DefaultOutputFormatter;
    13	
    14	impl InternalFormatter for DefaultOutputFormatter {
    15	    fn all_rules(&self, enabled_rules: FxHashSet<&str>) -> Option<String> {
    16	        let mut output = String::new();
    17	        let table = RuleTable::default();
    18	        for section in &table.sections {
    19	            output.push_str(&section.render_markdown_table_cli(&enabled_rules));
    20	            output.push('\n');
    21	        }
    22	        output.push_str(format!("Default: {}\n", table.turned_on_by_default_count).as_str());
    23	        output.push_str(format!("Total: {}\n", table.total).as_str());
    24	        Some(output)
    25	    }
    26	
    27	    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &super::LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
    28	        let time = Self::get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
    29	        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
    30	
    31	        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
    32	            Some(format!(
    33	                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
    34	                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
    35	            ))
    36	        } else {
    37	            Some(format!(
    38	                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
    39	                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
    40	            ))
    41	        }
    42	    }
    43	
    44	    #[cfg(not(any(test, feature = "testing")))]
    45	    fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
    46	        Box::new(GraphicalReporter::default())
    47	    }
    48	
    49	    #[cfg(any(test, feature = "testing"))]
    50	    fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
    51	        use crate::output_formatter::default::test_implementation::GraphicalReporterTester;
    52	
    53	        Box::new(GraphicalReporterTester::default())
    54	    }
    55	}
    56	
    57	impl DefaultOutputFormatter {
    58	    fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
    59	        let ms = duration.as_millis();
    60	        if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }
    61	    }
    62	}
    63	
    64	/// Pretty-prints diagnostics. Primarily meant for human-readable output in a terminal.
    65	///
    66	/// See [`GraphicalReportHandler`] for how to configure colors, context lines, etc.
    67	#[cfg_attr(all(not(test), feature = "testing"), expect(dead_code))]
    68	struct GraphicalReporter {
    69	    handler: GraphicalReportHandler,
    70	}
    71	
    72	impl Default for GraphicalReporter {
    73	    fn default() -> Self {
    74	        Self { handler: GraphicalReportHandler::new() }
    75	    }
    76	}
    77	
    78	impl DiagnosticReporter for GraphicalReporter {
    79	    fn finish(&mut self, result: &DiagnosticResult) -> Option<String> {
    80	        Some(get_diagnostic_result_output(result))
    81	    }
    82	
    83	    fn render_error(&mut self, error: Error) -> Option<String> {
    84	        let mut output = String::new();
    85	        self.handler.render_report(&mut output, error.as_ref()).unwrap();
    86	        Some(output)
    87	    }
    88	}
    89	
    90	pub(super) fn get_diagnostic_result_output(result: &DiagnosticResult) -> String {
    91	    let mut output = String::new();
    92	
    93	    if result.warnings_count() + result.errors_count() > 0 {
    94	        output.push('\n');
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    output.push_str(
    98	        format!(
    99	            "Found {} warning{} and {} error{}.\n",
   100	            result.warnings_count(),
   101	            if result.warnings_count() == 1 { "" } else { "s" },
   102	            result.errors_count(),
   103	            if result.errors_count() == 1 { "" } else { "s" },
   104	        )
   105	        .as_str(),
   106	    );
   107	
   108	    if result.max_warnings_exceeded() {
   109	        output.push_str(
   110	            format!("Exceeded maximum number of warnings. Found {}.\n", result.warnings_count())
   111	                .as_str(),
   112	        );
   113	    }
   114	
   115	    output
   116	}
   117	
   118	#[cfg(any(test, feature = "testing"))]
   119	mod test_implementation {
   120	    use oxc_diagnostics::{
   121	        Error, GraphicalReportHandler, GraphicalTheme,
   122	        reporter::{DiagnosticReporter, DiagnosticResult, Info},
   123	    };
   124	
   125	    use crate::output_formatter::default::get_diagnostic_result_output;
   126	
   127	    #[derive(Default)]
   128	    pub struct GraphicalReporterTester {
   129	        diagnostics: Vec<Error>,
   130	    }
   131	
   132	    impl DiagnosticReporter for GraphicalReporterTester {
   133	        fn finish(&mut self, result: &DiagnosticResult) -> Option<String> {
   134	            let handler = GraphicalReportHandler::new_themed(GraphicalTheme::none())
   135	                // links print ansi escape codes, which makes snapshots harder to read
   136	                .with_links(false);
   137	            let mut output = String::new();
   138	
   139	            self.diagnostics.sort_by_cached_key(|diagnostic| {
   140	                let info = Info::new(diagnostic);
   141	                (info.filename, info.start, info.end, info.rule_id, info.message)
   142	            });
   143	
   144	            for diagnostic in &self.diagnostics {
   145	                handler.render_report(&mut output, diagnostic.as_ref()).unwrap();
   146	            }
   147	
   148	            output.push_str(&get_diagnostic_result_output(result));
   149	
   150	            Some(output)
   151	        }
   152	
   153	        fn render_error(&mut self, error: Error) -> Option<String> {
   154	            self.diagnostics.push(error);
   155	            None
   156	        }
   157	    }
   158	}
   159

    31	pub struct CliRunner {
    32	    options: LintCommand,
    33	    cwd: PathBuf,
    34	    external_linter: Option<ExternalLinter>,
    35	    /// Callback for loading JavaScript/TypeScript config files (experimental).
    36	    /// This is only available when running via Node.js with NAPI.
    37	    #[cfg(feature = "napi")]
    38	    js_config_loader: Option<JsConfigLoaderCb>,
    39	}
    40	
    41	impl Debug for CliRunner {
    42	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    43	        let mut s = f.debug_struct("CliRunner");
    44	        s.field("options", &self.options).field("cwd", &self.cwd).field(
    45	            "external_linter",
    46	            if self.external_linter.is_some() { &"Some(ExternalLinter)" } else { &"None" },
    47	        );
    48	        #[cfg(feature = "napi")]
    49	        s.field(
    50	            "js_config_loader",
    51	            if self.js_config_loader.is_some() { &"Some(JsConfigLoaderCb)" } else { &"None" },
    52	        );
    53	        s.finish()
    54	    }
    55	}
    56	
    57	impl CliRunner {
    58	    /// # Panics
    59	    pub fn new(options: LintCommand, external_linter: Option<ExternalLinter>) -> Self {
    60	        Self {
    61	            options,
    62	            cwd: env::current_dir().expect("Failed to get current working directory"),
    63	            external_linter,
    64	            #[cfg(feature = "napi")]
    65	            js_config_loader: None,
    66	        }
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    /// # Panics
    70	    pub fn run(self, stdout: &mut dyn Write) -> CliRunResult {
    71	        let format_str = self.options.output_options.format;
    72	        let output_formatter = OutputFormatter::new(format_str);
    73	
    74	        let LintCommand {
    75	            paths,
    76	            filter,
    77	            basic_options,
    78	            warning_options,
    79	            ignore_options,
    80	            fix_options,
    81	            enable_plugins,
    82	            misc_options,
    83	            disable_nested_config,
    84	            inline_config_options,
    85	            ..
    86	        } = self.options;
    87	
    88	        if basic_options.init {
    89	            return crate::mode::run_init(&self.cwd, stdout);
    90	        }
    91	
    92	        let external_linter = self.external_linter.as_ref();
    93	
    94	        let mut paths = paths;
    95	        let provided_path_count = paths.len();
    96	        let now = Instant::now();
    97	
    98	        let filters = match Self::get_filters(filter) {
    99	            Ok(filters) => filters,
   100	            Err((result, message)) => {
   101	                print_and_flush_stdout(stdout, &message);
   102	                return result;
   103	            }
   104	        };
   105	
   106	        let handler = if cfg!(any(test, feature = "testing")) {
   107	            GraphicalReportHandler::new_themed(miette::GraphicalTheme::none())
   108	        } else {
   109	            GraphicalReportHandler::new()
   110	        };
   111	
   112	        let mut override_builder = None;
   113	
   114	        if !ignore_options.no_ignore {
   115	            let mut builder = OverrideBuilder::new(&self.cwd);
   116	
   117	            if !ignore_options.ignore_pattern.is_empty() {
   118	                for pattern in &ignore_options.ignore_pattern {
   119	                    // Meaning of ignore pattern is reversed
   120	                    // <https://docs.rs/ignore/latest/ignore/overrides/struct.OverrideBuilder.html#method.add>
   121	                    let pattern = format!("!{pattern}");
   122	                    builder.add(&pattern).unwrap();
   123	                }
   124	            }
   125	
   126	            let builder = builder.build().unwrap();
   127	
   128	            // The ignore crate whitelists explicit paths, but priority
   129	            // should be given to the ignore file. Many users lint
   130	            // automatically and pass a list of changed files explicitly.
   131	            // To accommodate this, unless `--no-ignore` is passed,
   132	            // pre-filter the paths.
   133	            if !paths.is_empty() {
   134	                let (ignore, _err) = Gitignore::new(&ignore_options.ignore_path);
   135	
   136	                paths.retain_mut(|p| {
   137	                    // Try to prepend cwd to all paths
   138	                    let Ok(mut path) = absolute(self.cwd.join(&p)) else {
   139	                        return false;
   140	                    };
   141	
   142	                    std::mem::swap(p, &mut path);
   143	
   144	                    if path.is_dir() {
   145	                        true
   146	                    } else {
   147	                        !(builder.matched(p, false).is_ignore()
   148	                            || ignore.matched(path, false).is_ignore())
   149	                    }
   150	                });
   151	            }
   152	
   153	            override_builder = Some(builder);
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        if paths.is_empty() {
   157	            // If explicit paths were provided, but all have been
   158	            // filtered, return early.
   159	            if provided_path_count > 0 {
   160	                return Self::handle_no_files_found(
   161	                    stdout,
   162	                    &output_formatter,
   163	                    now,
   164	                    None,
   165	                    misc_options.no_error_on_unmatched_pattern,
   166	                );
   167	            }
   168	
   169	            paths.push(self.cwd.clone());
   170	        }
   171	
   172	        let walker = Walk::new(&paths, &ignore_options, override_builder);
   173	        let mut paths = walker.paths();
   174	
   175	        // NAPI tests build `oxlint` with `testing` feature enabled.
   176	        // In NAPI tests, sort file paths if oxlint is run with `--threads 1`.
   177	        // This guarantees files are linted in a deterministic order.
   178	        //
   179	        // Note: Sorting paths would not be sufficient to guarantee deterministic linting order unless
   180	        // `--threads 1` is also used, because otherwise linting happens in parallel on multiple threads,
   181	        // which also produces non-determinism.
   182	        if cfg!(feature = "testing") && misc_options.threads == Some(1) {
   183	            paths.sort_unstable();
   184	        }
   185	
   186	        let mut external_plugin_store = ExternalPluginStore::new(self.external_linter.is_some());
   187	
   188	        // Setup JS workspace before loading any configs (config parsing can load JS plugins).
   189	        if let Some(external_linter) = &external_linter {
   190	            let res = (external_linter.create_workspace)(self.cwd.to_string_lossy().into_owned());
   191	
   192	            if let Err(err) = res {
   193	                print_and_flush_stdout(stdout, &format!("Failed to setup JS workspace:\n{err}\n"));
   194	                return CliRunResult::JsPluginWorkspaceSetupFailed;
   195	            }
   196	        }
   197	
   198	        let search_for_nested_configs = !disable_nested_config &&
   199	            // If the `--config` option is explicitly passed, we should not search for nested config files
   200	            // as the passed config file takes absolute precedence.
   201	            basic_options.config.is_none() &&
   202	            !misc_options.print_config &&
   203	            !self.options.list_rules;
   204	
   205	        let config_result = {
   206	            let mut config_loader =
   207	                ConfigLoader::new(external_linter, &mut external_plugin_store, &filters, None);
   208	            #[cfg(feature = "napi")]
   209	            {
   210	                config_loader = config_loader.with_js_config_loader(self.js_config_loader.as_ref());
   211	            }
   212	            config_loader.load_root_and_nested(
   213	                &self.cwd,
   214	                basic_options.config.as_ref(),
   215	                &paths,
   216	                search_for_nested_configs,
   217	            )
   218	        };
   219	
   220	        let (mut root_config, nested_configs, nested_ignore_patterns) = match config_result {
   221	            Ok(loaded) => (loaded.root, loaded.nested, loaded.nested_ignore_patterns),
   222	            Err(error) => {
   223	                match error {
   224	                    CliConfigLoadError::RootConfig(error) => {
   225	                        print_and_flush_stdout(
   226	                            stdout,
   227	                            &format!(
   228	                                "Failed to parse oxlint configuration file.\n{}\n",
   229	                                render_report(&handler, &error)
   230	                            ),
   231	                        );
   232	                    }
   233	                    CliConfigLoadError::NestedConfigs(errors) => {
   234	                        if let Some(error) = errors.into_iter().next() {
   235	                            let message = match &error {
   236	                                ConfigLoadError::Parse { path, error } => {
   237	                                    format!(
   238	                                        "Failed to parse oxlint configuration file at {}.\n{}\n",
   239	                                        path.to_string_lossy().cow_replace('\\', "/"),
   240	                                        render_report(&handler, error)
   241	                                    )
   242	                                }
   243	                                ConfigLoadError::Build { path, error } => {
   244	                                    format!(
   245	                                        "Failed to build configuration from {}.\n{}\n",
   246	                                        path.to_string_lossy().cow_replace('\\', "/"),
   247	                                        render_report(
   248	                                            &handler,
   249	                                            &OxcDiagnostic::error(error.clone())
   250	                                        )
   251	                                    )
   252	                                }
   253	                                ConfigLoadError::JsConfigFileFoundButJsRuntimeNotAvailable => {
   254	                                    "Error: JavaScript/TypeScript config files found but JS runtime not available.\n\
   255	                                     This is an experimental feature that requires running oxlint via Node.js.\n\
   256	                                     Please use JSON config files (.oxlintrc.json or .oxlintrc.jsonc) instead, or run oxlint via the npm package.\n".to_string()
   257	                                }
   258	                                ConfigLoadError::Diagnostic(error) => {
   259	                                    let report = render_report(&handler, error);
   260	                                    format!("Failed to parse oxlint configuration file.\n{report}\n")
   261	                                }
   262	                            };
   263	                            print_and_flush_stdout(stdout, &message);
   264	                        }
   265	                    }
   266	                }
   267	
   268	                return CliRunResult::InvalidOptionConfig;
   269	            }
   270	        };
   271	
   272	        {
   273	            let mut plugins = root_config.plugins.unwrap_or_default();
   274	            enable_plugins.apply_overrides(&mut plugins);
   275	            root_config.plugins = Some(plugins);
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        let base_ignore_patterns = root_config.ignore_patterns.clone();
   279	
   280	        let config_builder = match ConfigStoreBuilder::from_oxlintrc(
   281	            false,
   282	            root_config.clone(),
   283	            external_linter,
   284	            &mut external_plugin_store,
   285	            None,
   286	        ) {
   287	            Ok(builder) => builder,
   288	            Err(e) => {
   289	                print_and_flush_stdout(
   290	                    stdout,
   291	                    &format!(
   292	                        "Failed to parse oxlint configuration file.\n{}\n",
   293	                        render_config_builder_error(&handler, e)
   294	                    ),
   295	                );
   296	                return CliRunResult::InvalidOptionConfig;
   297	            }
   298	        }
   299	        .with_filters(&filters);
   300	
   301	        if misc_options.print_config {
   302	            return crate::mode::run_print_config(&config_builder, root_config, stdout);
   303	        }
   304	
   305	        let lint_config = match config_builder.build(&mut external_plugin_store) {
   306	            Ok(config) => config,
   307	            Err(e) => {
   308	                print_and_flush_stdout(
   309	                    stdout,
   310	                    &format!(
   311	                        "Failed to build configuration.\n{}\n",
   312	                        render_config_builder_error(&handler, e)
   313	                    ),
   314	                );
   315	                return CliRunResult::InvalidOptionConfig;
   316	            }
   317	        };
   318	
   319	        if self.options.list_rules {
   320	            return crate::mode::run_rules(&lint_config, &output_formatter, stdout);
   321	        }
   322	
   323	        let ignore_matcher =
   324	            { LintIgnoreMatcher::new(&base_ignore_patterns, &self.cwd, nested_ignore_patterns) };
   325	
   326	        // If no external rules, discard `ExternalLinter`
   327	        let mut external_linter = self.external_linter;
   328	        if external_plugin_store.is_empty() {
   329	            external_linter = None;
   330	        }
   331	
   332	        // TODO(refactor): pull this into a shared function, so that the language server can use
   333	        // the same functionality.
   334	        let use_cross_module = lint_config.plugins().has_import()
   335	            || nested_configs.values().any(|config| config.plugins().has_import());
   336	        let mut options =
   337	            LintServiceOptions::new(self.cwd.clone()).with_cross_module(use_cross_module);
   338	
   339	        let config_store = ConfigStore::new(lint_config, nested_configs, external_plugin_store);
   340	        let type_check_only = self.options.type_check_only;
   341	        let type_aware =
   342	            type_check_only || self.options.type_aware || config_store.type_aware_enabled();
   343	        let type_check =
   344	            type_check_only || self.options.type_check || config_store.type_check_enabled();
   345	        if type_check && !type_aware {
   346	            print_and_flush_stdout(
   347	                stdout,
   348	                "The `--type-check` option requires type-aware linting.\nUse `--type-aware --type-check` or enable `options.typeAware` in your config.\n",
   349	            );
   350	            return CliRunResult::InvalidOptionTypeCheckWithoutTypeAware;
   351	        }
   352	        if type_check_only && fix_options.is_enabled() {
   353	            print_and_flush_stdout(
   354	                stdout,
   355	                "The `--type-check-only` option cannot be used with fix flags.\nRemove `--fix`, `--fix-suggestions`, and `--fix-dangerously`.\n",
   356	            );
   357	            return CliRunResult::InvalidOptionTypeCheckOnlyWithFix;
   358	        }
   359	        let deny_warnings = warning_options.deny_warnings || config_store.deny_warnings();
   360	        let max_warnings = warning_options.max_warnings.or(config_store.max_warnings());
   361	
   362	        // Only propagate Warn/Deny; treat Allow (off) as disabling reports.
   363	        let report_unused_directives = if type_check_only {
   364	            None
   365	        } else {
   366	            match inline_config_options.report_unused_directives {
   367	                ReportUnusedDirectives::WithoutSeverity(true) => Some(AllowWarnDeny::Warn),
   368	                ReportUnusedDirectives::WithSeverity(Some(severity)) if severity.is_warn_deny() => {
   369	                    Some(severity)
   370	                }
   371	                ReportUnusedDirectives::WithSeverity(Some(_)) => None,
   372	                _ => match config_store.report_unused_disable_directives() {
   373	                    Some(severity) if severity.is_warn_deny() => Some(severity),
   374	                    _ => None,
   375	                },
   376	            }
   377	        };
   378	        let (mut diagnostic_service, tx_error) = Self::get_diagnostic_service(
   379	            &output_formatter,
   380	            &warning_options,
   381	            &misc_options,
   382	            max_warnings,
   383	        );
   384	
   385	        // Send JS plugins config to JS side
   386	        if let Some(external_linter) = &external_linter {
   387	            let res = config_store.external_plugin_store().setup_rule_configs(
   388	                self.cwd.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
   389	                None,
   390	                external_linter,
   391	            );
   392	            if let Err(err) = res {
   393	                print_and_flush_stdout(

   495	impl CliRunner {
   496	    #[must_use]
   497	    pub fn with_cwd(mut self, cwd: PathBuf) -> Self {
   498	        self.cwd = cwd;
   499	        self
   500	    }
   501	
   502	    #[cfg(feature = "napi")]
   503	    #[must_use]
   504	    pub fn with_config_loader(mut self, config_loader: Option<JsConfigLoaderCb>) -> Self {
   505	        self.js_config_loader = config_loader;
   506	        self
   507	    }
   508	
   509	    fn get_diagnostic_service(
   510	        reporter: &OutputFormatter,
   511	        warning_options: &WarningOptions,
   512	        misc_options: &MiscOptions,
   513	        max_warnings: Option<usize>,
   514	    ) -> (DiagnosticService, DiagnosticSender) {
   515	        let (service, sender) = DiagnosticService::new(reporter.get_diagnostic_reporter());
   516	        (
   517	            service
   518	                .with_quiet(warning_options.quiet)
   519	                .with_silent(misc_options.silent)
   520	                .with_max_warnings(max_warnings),
   521	            sender,
   522	        )
   523	    }
   524	
   525	    fn handle_no_files_found(
   526	        stdout: &mut dyn Write,
   527	        output_formatter: &OutputFormatter,
   528	        now: Instant,
   529	        number_of_rules: Option<usize>,
   530	        no_error_on_unmatched_pattern: bool,
   531	    ) -> CliRunResult {
   532	        if !no_error_on_unmatched_pattern {
   533	            print_and_flush_stdout(
   534	                stdout,
   535	                "No files found to lint. Please check your paths and ignore patterns.\n",
   536	            );
   537	        }
   538	
   539	        if let Some(end) = output_formatter.lint_command_info(&LintCommandInfo {
   540	            number_of_files: 0,
   541	            number_of_rules,
   542	            threads_count: rayon::current_num_threads(),
   543	            start_time: now.elapsed(),
   544	        }) {
   545	            print_and_flush_stdout(stdout, &end);
   546	        }
   547	
   548	        if no_error_on_unmatched_pattern {
   549	            CliRunResult::LintSucceeded
   550	        } else {
   551	            CliRunResult::LintNoFilesFound
   552	        }
   553	    }

Now I understand the issue. The GitHub output formatter doesn't implement `lint_command_info` method from the `InternalFormatter` trait. Looking at the trait definition (line 81-83 in mod.rs):

```rust
fn lint_command_info(&self, _lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
    None
}
```

The default implementation returns `None`, which means no summary info is printed. The `DefaultOutputFormatter` overrides it to return the summary like "Finished in 1.9s on 850 files with 312 rules using 28 threads."

The GitHub formatter needs to also implement `lint_command_info` to return the same summary.

So the default format has the "Finished in ..." line. The GitHub format should also have it.

Now let me implement the fix. The GitHub output formatter needs to implement `lint_command_info` to return the same summary as the default formatter.

Looking at the default formatter's implementation:

```rust
fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &super::LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
    let time = Self::get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
    let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };

    if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
        Some(format!(
            "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
            lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
        ))
    } else {
        Some(format!(
            "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
            lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
        ))
    }
}
```

I need to add the same method to the `GithubOutputFormatter`. Let me also check the `get_execution_time` function - it's defined on `DefaultOutputFormatter`. I could either:
1. Move it to a shared location
2. Re-implement it in the github module
3. Make it a standalone function

```rust
use super::default::get_diagnostic_result_output;
```

```rust
impl DefaultOutputFormatter {
    fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
        let ms = duration.as_millis();
        if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }
    }
}
```

This is a private method on `DefaultOutputFormatter`. I can either:
1. Make it a pub function in `default.rs` and use it from `github.rs`
2. Duplicate it in `github.rs`

    13	
    14	impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {

    24	        } else {

    26	                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
    27	                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
    28	            ))
    29	        }
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
    33	        Box::new(GithubReporter)
    34	    }
    35	}
    36	
    37	/// Formats reports using [GitHub Actions
    38	/// annotations](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-commands-for-github-actions#setting-an-error-message). Useful for reporting in CI.
    39	struct GithubReporter;
    40	
    41	impl DiagnosticReporter for GithubReporter {
    42	    fn finish(&mut self, result: &DiagnosticResult) -> Option<String> {
    43	        Some(get_diagnostic_result_output(result))
    44	    }
    45	
    46	    fn supports_minified_file_fallback(&self) -> bool {
    47	        false
    48	    }
    49	
    50	    fn render_error(&mut self, error: Error) -> Option<String> {
    51	        Some(format_github(&error))
    52	    }
    53	}
    54	
    55	fn format_github(diagnostic: &Error) -> String {
    56	    let Info { start, end, filename, message, severity, rule_id } = Info::new(diagnostic);
    57	    let severity = match severity {
    58	        Severity::Error => "error",
    59	        Severity::Warning | miette::Severity::Advice => "warning",
    60	    };
    61	    let title = rule_id.map_or(Cow::Borrowed("oxlint"), Cow::Owned);
    62	    let filename = escape_property(&filename);
    63	    let message = escape_data(&message);
    64	    format!(
    65	        "::{severity} file={filename},line={},endLine={},col={},endColumn={},title={title}::{message}\n",
    66	        start.line, end.line, start.column, end.column
    67	    )
    68	}
    69	
    70	fn escape_data(value: &str) -> String {
    71	    // Refs:
    72	    // - https://github.com/actions/runner/blob/a4c57f27477077e57545af79851551ff7f5632bd/src/Runner.Common/ActionCommand.cs#L18-L22
    73	    // - https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/fe3e7ce9a7f995d29d1fcfd226a32bca407f9dc8/packages/core/src/command.ts#L80-L94
    74	    let mut result = String::with_capacity(value.len());
    75	    for c in value.chars() {
    76	        match c {
    77	            '\r' => result.push_str("%0D"),
    78	            '\n' => result.push_str("%0A"),
    79	            '%' => result.push_str("%25"),
    80	            _ => result.push(c),
    81	        }
    82	    }
    83	    result
    84	}
    85	
    86	fn escape_property(value: &str) -> String {
    87	    // Refs:
    88	    // - https://github.com/actions/runner/blob/a4c57f27477077e57545af79851551ff7f5632bd/src/Runner.Common/ActionCommand.cs#L25-L32
    89	    // - https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/fe3e7ce9a7f995d29d1fcfd226a32bca407f9dc8/packages/core/src/command.ts#L80-L94
    90	    let mut result = String::with_capacity(value.len());
    91	    for c in value.chars() {
    92	        match c {
    93	            '\r' => result.push_str("%0D"),
    94	            '\n' => result.push_str("%0A"),
    95	            ':' => result.push_str("%3A"),
    96	            ',' => result.push_str("%2C"),
    97	            '%' => result.push_str("%25"),
    98	            _ => result.push(c),
    99	        }
   100	    }
   101	    result
   102	}
   103	
   104	#[cfg(test)]
   105	mod test {
   106	    use oxc_diagnostics::{
   107	        DiagnosticService, NamedSource, OxcDiagnostic,
   108	        reporter::{DiagnosticReporter, DiagnosticResult},
   109	    };
   110	    use oxc_span::Span;
   111	
   112	    use super::GithubReporter;
   113	
   114	    #[test]
   115	    fn reporter_finish() {
   116	        let mut reporter = GithubReporter;
   117	
   118	        let result = reporter.finish(&DiagnosticResult::default());
   119	
   120	        assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), "Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.\n");
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    #[test]
   124	    fn reporter_finish_with_errors() {
   125	        let mut reporter = GithubReporter;
   126	
   127	        let result = reporter.finish(&DiagnosticResult::new(2, 1, false));
   128	
   129	        assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), "\nFound 2 warnings and 1 error.\n");
   130	    }
   131	
   132	    #[test]
   133	    fn reporter_error() {
   134	        let mut reporter = GithubReporter;
   135	        let error = OxcDiagnostic::warn("error message")
   136	            .with_label(Span::new(0, 8))
   137	            .with_source_code(NamedSource::new("file://test.ts", "debugger;"));
   138	
   139	        let result = reporter.render_error(error);
   140	
   141	        assert!(result.is_some());
   142	        assert_eq!(
   143	            result.unwrap(),
   144	            "::warning file=file%3A//test.ts,line=1,endLine=1,col=1,endColumn=9,title=oxlint::error message\n"
   145	        );
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    #[test]
   149	    fn reporter_does_not_use_minified_fallback_for_long_annotations() {
   150	        let source_text = format!("{}\n", "a".repeat(1300));
   151	        let diagnostic = OxcDiagnostic::warn("error message")
   152	            .with_label(Span::new(0, 1300))
   153	            .with_source_code(NamedSource::new("file://test.ts", source_text));
   154	
   155	        let (mut service, sender) = DiagnosticService::new(Box::new(GithubReporter));
   156	        sender.send(vec![diagnostic]).unwrap();
   157	        drop(sender);
   158	
   159	        let mut output = Vec::new();
   160	        service.run(&mut output);
   161	        let output = String::from_utf8(output).unwrap();
   162	
   163	        assert!(output.starts_with("::warning file=file%3A//test.ts,line=1,endLine=1,col=1,"));
   164	        assert!(output.contains("title=oxlint::error message"));
   165	        assert!(!output.contains("File is too long to fit on the screen"));
   166	        assert!(!output.contains("file=,line=0,endLine=0,col=0,endColumn=0"));
   167	    }
   168	}
   169

    58	    fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
    59	        get_execution_time(duration)
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	pub(super) fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
    64	    let ms = duration.as_millis();
    65	    if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }
    66	}

    11	#[derive(Debug)]
    12	pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
    13	
    14	impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
    15	    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
    16	        let time = get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
    17	        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
    18	
    19	        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
    20	            Some(format!(
    21	                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
    22	                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
    23	            ))
    24	        } else {
    25	            Some(format!(
    26	                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
    27	                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
    28	            ))
    29	        }
    30	    }

// Simulate what the github output format would look like
fn get_execution_time(ms: u128) -> String {
    if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", ms as f64 / 1000.0) }
}

fn main() {
    // Test case 1: 0 warnings, 0 errors
    println!("Case 1: 0 warnings, 0 errors on 850 files with 312 rules");
    println!("Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.");
    println!("Finished in {} on {} files with {} rules using {} threads.", get_execution_time(1900), 850, 312, 28);
    
    // Test case 2: with errors
    println!("\nCase 2: with errors");
    println!("Found 2 warnings and 1 error.");
    println!("Finished in {} on {} files with {} rules using {} threads.", get_execution_time(150), 3, 10, 4);
    
    // Test case 3: single file
    println!("\nCase 3: single file");
    println!("Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.");
    println!("Finished in {} on {} file with {} rules using {} threads.", get_execution_time(50), 1, 10, 4);
    
    // Test case 4: unknown number of rules
    println!("\nCase 4: unknown rules");
    println!("Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.");
    println!("Finished in {} on {} files using {} threads.", get_execution_time(500), 10, 4);

+use crate::output_formatter::{InternalFormatter, LintCommandInfo};
 
 #[derive(Debug)]
 pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
 
 impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
+    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
+        let time = get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
+        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
+
+        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        } else {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        }
+    }
+

         Box::new(GithubReporter)
     }

+pub(super) fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
+    let ms = duration.as_millis();
+    if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }
+}

diff --git a/apps/oxlint/src/output_formatter/github.rs b/apps/oxlint/src/output_formatter/github.rs
index 16fcbcb..21dd325 100644
--- a/apps/oxlint/src/output_formatter/github.rs

@@ -5,13 +5,30 @@ use oxc_diagnostics::{
     reporter::{DiagnosticReporter, DiagnosticResult, Info},

-use super::default::get_diagnostic_result_output;

+use super::default::{get_diagnostic_result_output, get_execution_time};
+use crate::output_formatter::{InternalFormatter, LintCommandInfo};
 
 #[derive(Debug)]
 pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
 
 impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
+    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
+        let time = get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
+        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
+
+        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        } else {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        }
+    }
+
     fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
         Box::new(GithubReporter)
     }

+pub(super) fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
+    let ms = duration.as_millis();
+    if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }

-use super::default::get_diagnostic_result_output;

+use super::default::{get_diagnostic_result_output, get_execution_time};
+use crate::output_formatter::{InternalFormatter, LintCommandInfo};
 
 #[derive(Debug)]
 pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
 
 impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
+    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
+        let time = get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
+        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
+
+        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        } else {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        }
+    }
+
     fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
         Box::new(GithubReporter)
     }

+pub(super) fn get_execution_time(duration: &Duration) -> String {
+    let ms = duration.as_millis();
+    if ms < 1000 { format!("{ms}ms") } else { format!("{:.1}s", duration.as_secs_f64()) }
+}

+use super::default::{get_diagnostic_result_output, get_execution_time};
+use crate::output_formatter::{InternalFormatter, LintCommandInfo};
 
 #[derive(Debug)]
 pub struct GithubOutputFormatter;
 
 impl InternalFormatter for GithubOutputFormatter {
+    fn lint_command_info(&self, lint_command_info: &LintCommandInfo) -> Option<String> {
+        let time = get_execution_time(&lint_command_info.start_time);
+        let s = if lint_command_info.number_of_files == 1 { "" } else { "s" };
+
+        if let Some(number_of_rules) = lint_command_info.number_of_rules {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} with {} rules using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, number_of_rules, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        } else {
+            Some(format!(
+                "Finished in {time} on {} file{s} using {} threads.\n",
+                lint_command_info.number_of_files, lint_command_info.threads_count
+            ))
+        }
+    }
+
     fn get_diagnostic_reporter(&self) -> Box<dyn DiagnosticReporter> {
         Box::new(GithubReporter)
     }
